skill.name: obsidian-plan-wiki
skill.description: Create and maintain modular Obsidian-compatible project plan wikis with linked markdown files, task comments, changelog entries, and research notes.
skill.summary: Build structured Obsidian plan wikis for projects, roadmaps, research, and implementation tasks.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are markdown examples, code fences, Obsidian wiki links, and template text rather than executable code. The skill does guide agents to create and update local markdown files and includes a local grep command example, so the residual risk is limited to expected filesystem work and optional local search.

low_findings.title: Local Markdown File Modification
low_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create directories, write README, CLAUDE, changelog, phase, task, and research markdown files. This is the core purpose of the skill, but users should apply it only inside the intended plan workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and update behavior is explicit in the workflow steps. It is legitimate for this documentation skill and does not show destructive intent.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Search Command Example
low_findings.description: The skill includes grep examples for finding open Obsidian task comments. The command is a local search pattern in documentation, not hidden execution or an exfiltration path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The grep command is directly shown in markdown and has a clear project-management purpose. It still represents optional external command guidance, so it remains a low-risk finding.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Syntax
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, path traversal, and most Ruby or shell backtick findings are caused by markdown code fences, .md file names, Obsidian wiki links, relative links, and inline code formatting. No executable script, dynamic command runner, credential access, or network request was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear in markdown examples and documentation templates. The reviewed files are markdown only and contain no executable implementation layer.

content.value_statement: Large project plans become hard to navigate when every detail lives in one document. This skill creates linked Obsidian markdown wikis with focused task files, research notes, and changelog history.
actual_capabilities: Creates a modular markdown plan structure for Obsidian.
Defines README, CLAUDE, changelog, task, phase, reference, and research files.
Uses Obsidian wiki links for navigation between plan files.
Tracks open questions with hidden Obsidian comment checkboxes.
Supports research notes and Delphi-style synthesis workflows.
Maintains changelog entries for plan amendments.
limitations: It does not include executable scripts or an Obsidian plugin.
It depends on the agent to create and update files correctly.
It does not validate wiki links automatically.
It is best for markdown-based planning, not runtime project management.

use_cases.title: Create a Product Roadmap Wiki
use_cases.description: Turn a broad roadmap into linked phase files, task pages, research notes, and a clear changelog.

use_cases.title: Organize Engineering Implementation Plans
use_cases.description: Break implementation work into focused task documents with files, steps, success criteria, and research links.

use_cases.title: Maintain Research-Backed Documentation
use_cases.description: Capture open questions, research outputs, decisions, and deferred work inside an Obsidian-compatible knowledge base.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Plan Wiki
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Obsidian plan wiki for this project. Include a README, changelog, phases, tasks, reference notes, and research index.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Roadmap into Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this roadmap into an Obsidian plan wiki. Split it into phase files and task files with success criteria and wiki links.

prompt_templates.title: Process Open Research Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the open Obsidian task comments in this plan. Identify which questions need research and create linked research notes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an Existing Plan Wiki
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this existing plan wiki for overloaded files, missing changelog entries, weak links, deferred work, and unclear task boundaries.

output_examples.input: Create a plan wiki for a mobile app redesign.
output: A README that summarizes the goal, tech stack, phases, and task index.
Separate phase and task files for discovery, design, implementation, testing, and launch.
A changelog entry for the initial plan structure and a research index for open questions.

output_examples.input: Add research tracking to an existing project plan.
output: Hidden Obsidian task comments for unanswered decisions.
Linked research notes for each completed investigation.
Updated task files that point to the research results and mark questions as complete.

output_examples.input: Clean up a long planning document.
output: A smaller index page with links to focused topic files.
Dedicated task files with owners, steps, and success criteria.
A preserved deferred-work file for items removed from the active plan.

best_practices: Keep each markdown file focused on one phase, task, decision, or research topic.
Update the changelog immediately after creating or changing plan files.
Use Obsidian wiki links consistently so readers can navigate without searching.
anti_patterns: Do not place the entire project plan in one large markdown file.
Do not delete deferred work when it can be preserved for later review.
Do not mark research questions complete without linking to the result.

faq.question: Does this skill require Obsidian?
faq.answer: No. It writes standard markdown, but the link format is designed for Obsidian navigation.

faq.question: Can it create project files outside the wiki?
faq.answer: No. It is intended to create planning and documentation files, not application source code.

faq.question: How are tasks tracked?
faq.answer: Tasks and open questions use hidden Obsidian comments with checkbox markers.

faq.question: Does it run automation scripts?
faq.answer: No scripts are included. The documentation shows a local grep command for finding open task comments.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing plan wiki?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect the current structure, add missing links, and improve task organization.

faq.question: What makes the output different from a normal roadmap?
faq.answer: The output is a linked markdown knowledge base with phase pages, task pages, research notes, and changelog history.
